data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-12T15:49:30.635Z</current_datetime>

I'm writing MSTest tests for a method that returns a list. What MSTest collection assertions should I use instead of Assert.IsTrue? Here's what I have:

```csharp
[TestMethod]
public void GetActiveUsers_ReturnsOnlyActiveUsers()
{
    var service = new UserService();
    var users = service.GetActiveUsers();

    Assert.IsTrue(users.Count > 0);
    Assert.IsTrue(users.All(u => u.IsActive));
    Assert.IsTrue(users.Count() == 3);
    var admin = users.Single(u => u.Role == "Admin");
    Assert.IsTrue(admin != null);
}
```

data.content:
Here are the recommended replacements:

```csharp
[TestMethod]
public void GetActiveUsers_ReturnsOnlyActiveUsers()
{
    var service = new UserService();
    var users = service.GetActiveUsers();

    Assert.IsTrue(users.Count > 0);                    // ✅ already fine, or use:
    // CollectionAssert.That.IsNotEmpty(users);         //    (MSTest v3.7+)

    CollectionAssert.AllItemsAreNotNull(users);         // no nulls
    Assert.IsTrue(users.All(u => u.IsActive));          // ✅ no built-in predicate overload

    Assert.AreEqual(3, users.Count);                    // exact count check

    var admin = users.Single(u => u.Role == "Admin");
    Assert.IsNotNull(admin);                            // null check
}
```

**Key MSTest collection assertions:**

| Instead of | Use |
|---|---|
| `Assert.IsTrue(x != null)` | `Assert.IsNotNull(x)` |
| `Assert.IsTrue(list.Count == n)` | `Assert.AreEqual(n, list.Count)` |
| `Assert.IsTrue(list.Contains(x))` | `CollectionAssert.Contains(list, x)` |
| check all items non-null | `CollectionAssert.AllItemsAreNotNull(list)` |
| check all items unique | `CollectionAssert.AllItemsAreUnique(list)` |
| check exact contents (order-insensitive) | `CollectionAssert.AreEquivalent(expected, actual)` |
| check exact contents (ordered) | `CollectionAssert.AreEqual(expected, actual)` |

**Note:** `CollectionAssert` doesn't have a predicate-based `All` method, so `Assert.IsTrue(users.All(u => u.IsActive))` is the standard approach there. If you upgrade to **MSTest v3.7+**, the new `Assert.That` API adds `Assert.That.AllSatisfy(users, u => Assert.IsTrue(u.IsActive))` for better failure messages.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about MSTest collection assertions to replace their `Assert.IsTrue` calls. This is a general knowledge question about MSTest - I don't need to search any codebase.
